title: Bright-and-Airy Lightroom Grade for a 38-Shot Food Photography Set (Flat-Lay, 45°, Side)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief (db_i 1113): a Lightroom-editor food-photography job needing "color correction, white balance, exposure to create a cohesive presentation", "Bright and Airy Food Photography is the goal", a set of "35/40 photos of one subject with flat lay, 45 degree and side shots", photos "close to the goal but need tweaking", and an "ASAP / Quick Turnaround" deadline. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable, gradeable Adobe-connector benchmark: I fixed the set at 38 frames and named three representative hero frames (a flat-lay, a 45°, and a side shot) that the chain actually operates on (the connector edits per-image, so a benchmark exercises the recipe on representative frames and states it is replayed across the remaining 35); I specified the concrete bright-and-airy recipe as discrete connector ops (warm the white balance, lift exposure, recover plate/window highlights, open the shadows, gentle vibrance, a selective lift of the dish itself via a prompt-mask, light de-haze contrast); I added an auto-straighten on the flat-lay and a crop/resize export pass because the client wants delivery-ready files; and I added a stock linen-backdrop license + an Adobe Express social-teaser sizing step as a realistic upsell. I did NOT invent any new deliverable, re-shoot, composite, or change the subject — it remains a pure tonal/color cohesive edit of the client's own frames, exactly as the brief asks. No generative fill, no object removal, no background replacement are used anywhere.
one_line_ask: Take a client's 38-frame food shoot (flat-lay / 45° / side of one dish) that is close to the mark and grade it to a cohesive bright-and-airy look — white balance, exposure, highlight/shadow, gentle color — then deliver straightened, correctly-sized final files.
full_brief:
A food-photography client (single hero dish, shot three ways: an overhead flat-lay, a 45-degree three-quarter, and a side/profile shot, 38 frames total) hands you a set that is "close to the goal but needs tweaking." The goal is a consistent BRIGHT AND AIRY look across the whole set: clean neutral-to-slightly-warm white balance, lifted exposure, bright but not blown highlights on the plate and window light, open (not muddy) shadows, and gentle, appetising color — applied cohesively so the 38 frames read as one shoot. Quick turnaround / ASAP.

Concretely, working in the Adobe connector entirely on the client's own pixels (no compositing, no generative fill, no background swap):
1. Ingest the three representative hero frames the recipe is tuned on.
2. On the flat-lay, square it up (auto-straighten the overhead so the plate edges are true) and crop to a clean composition.
3. Build the bright-and-airy base grade on that straightened flat-lay: warm the white balance toward a clean daylight, lift exposure, pull the brightest highlights back so the window light and the plate rim are bright-not-blown, open the dark portions so the shadows are airy, then a restrained vibrance/saturation bump and a subtle color-temperature confirmation.
4. Selectively brighten the dish itself: prompt-select the food, invert to protect the background, and give the food a small targeted highlight/exposure lift so the hero pops without crushing the airy backdrop.
5. Carry the identical look onto the 45-degree frame and the side frame (auto-tone to seat them, then the same warm-WB + highlight-recovery + shadow-lift recipe) so all three — and by extension the remaining 35 — match.
6. Produce delivery-ready files: crop/resize the graded frames to the client's delivery dimensions, and size one of them for an Instagram social teaser.

Deliverables: the three fully-graded hero frames at delivery resolution, the selective-food-pop master, an Instagram-sized teaser, and a short edit recipe note describing the cohesive look so the client can see exactly how the set was unified.

inputs.name: flatlay_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Overhead flat-lay food photograph of a single plated brunch dish — ricotta toast with figs, honey drizzle and microgreens on a matte off-white ceramic plate, on a pale oak table with a folded linen napkin, a small espresso cup and scattered fig halves around it. Shot slightly UNDER the bright-and-airy target: a touch cool/blue in white balance, exposure about half a stop low, shadows slightly heavy under the plate, highlights on the honey not yet luminous. Natural soft window light from upper-left, real documentary food-photography look, genuine crumbs and honey sheen, real ceramic texture, NOT glossy CGI, very slight 1-2 degree rotation off-square so it needs straightening. 3000x4000 portrait.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real, almost-finished food frame straight out of Lightroom import — faintly cool, faintly dark, slightly tilted — so the grading chain has real work to do. Genuine crumbs, honey sheen, ceramic pores; avoid plastic CGI sheen.

inputs.name: angle45_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: 45-degree three-quarter food photograph of the SAME ricotta-fig honey toast on the same matte off-white plate, same pale oak table and linen napkin, same espresso cup in soft background bokeh. Same slightly-cool, slightly-underexposed near-miss grade as the flat-lay so the set is consistent but needs the same tweak. Soft window light upper-left, shallow depth of field, real food texture, candid documentary style, no CGI gloss. 4000x3000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Same dish/props/light as the flat-lay so a cohesive grade is meaningfully testable. Same near-miss white balance and exposure so the matching recipe is justified.

inputs.name: side_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Side/profile eye-level food photograph of the SAME ricotta-fig honey toast, showing the layered height of the toast, fig halves and honey dripping over the edge, same off-white plate, same pale oak table, blurred bright window in the background. Same near-target grade: marginally cool white balance, half-stop low exposure, slightly heavy shadows, window highlights a little flat. Real documentary food look, authentic honey drip and crumb, no CGI sheen. 4000x3000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Profile angle of the identical scene; the bright background window gives the highlight-recovery step something genuine to recover. Keep it photoreal, not glossy.

outputs.name: flatlay_bright_airy.jpg
outputs.spec: Straightened, cropped, fully bright-and-airy-graded flat-lay at client delivery resolution (3000px long edge, sRGB JPG).

outputs.name: food_pop_master.jpg
outputs.spec: The flat-lay grade with a selective targeted lift on the dish (food prompt-selected, background protected) — the hero master, full resolution sRGB JPG.

outputs.name: angle45_bright_airy.jpg
outputs.spec: 45-degree frame matched to the same bright-and-airy look (auto-tone + warm WB + highlight recovery + shadow lift), delivery resolution sRGB JPG.

outputs.name: side_bright_airy.jpg
outputs.spec: Side frame matched to the same look, delivery resolution sRGB JPG.

outputs.name: ig_teaser_1080.jpg
outputs.spec: Instagram teaser crop/resize of the food-pop master at 1080x1350 (4:5) sRGB JPG.

outputs.name: edit_recipe_note.txt
outputs.spec: Short plain-text note documenting the cohesive bright-and-airy recipe (the ordered list of adjustments and their intent) so the client can see how the set was unified; compiled locally from the connector step log.

connector_workflow.note: Open an upload session for the client's three hero frames (flatlay_raw.jpg, angle45_raw.jpg, side_raw.jpg). Initial asset load — the only step with no upstream connector input.
Finalize the upload; yields stored asset references/URLs for the three raw frames that every edit step downstream consumes.
Square up the overhead flat-lay so the plate edges and table grain are true — the slight 1-2 degree tilt is corrected before composition.
Crop the straightened flat-lay to a clean composition (trim the rotation margins, tighten around the plate).
Warm the white balance off the faintly-cool import toward a clean daylight — the core of the bright-and-airy look.
Lift overall exposure ~half a stop to brighten the frame (airy base).
Pull the brightest highlights back so the window light and honey/plate-rim are bright-not-blown after the exposure lift.
Open the dark portions (shadows under the plate) so they read airy, not muddy — completes the bright-and-airy tonal base.
Restrained vibrance/saturation bump for appetising-but-not-garish color. This is the finished flat-lay base grade (deliverable flatlay_bright_airy.jpg) and the reference look for the other frames.
Prompt-select the food (the toast and figs) on the graded flat-lay to build a mask for a targeted pop.
Invert the food mask so the next lift protects the airy background and acts only on the dish (background-protected).
Apply a small targeted lift to the food's light portions through the food mask so the hero dish pops without brightening the protected background. Deliverable food_pop_master.jpg.
Seat the 45-degree frame with auto-tone as a consistent starting point before matching the reference recipe.
Apply the same warm white-balance shift used on the flat-lay so the 45-degree frame matches the set.
Recover highlights + (with the matching exposure/shadow intent baked from auto-tone) finish the 45-degree frame to the bright-and-airy look. Deliverable angle45_bright_airy.jpg.
Seat the side/profile frame with auto-tone before applying the matching recipe.
Open the shadows on the side frame to match the airy base (its bright background window already handled by auto-tone highlight roll-off), producing the matched side deliverable side_bright_airy.jpg.
Crop/resize the food-pop master to 1080x1350 (4:5) for the Instagram teaser deliverable ig_teaser_1080.jpg.
Assemble the graded deliverables (already produced above) into a Firefly board deep-link so the client can review the cohesive set in one place — presents existing outputs, generates no new art.
Single unavoidable local step: compile the ordered connector adjustments into edit_recipe_note.txt so the client has a written record of the cohesive look. No core editing done locally.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-2 ingest the client's raw frames (the only no-input load). The flat-lay pipeline is a single unbroken chain: ingested_frames → auto_straighten (flatlay_straight) → crop_to_bounds (flatlay_cropped) → color_temperature (flatlay_wb) → exposure (flatlay_exposed) → highlights (flatlay_highlights) → dark_portions (flatlay_shadows) → vibrance (flatlay_bright_airy). That finished grade feeds the selective pop: select_by_prompt (food_mask) → invert_selection (bg_mask), and adjust_light_portions consumes BOTH flatlay_bright_airy AND food_mask to produce food_pop_master. The 45° and side frames each branch from ingested_frames through their own auto_tone → WB/highlight/shadow sub-chains to match the look. crop_and_resize consumes food_pop_master to make the IG teaser. create_firefly_board consumes the five finished deliverables. The lone local step consumes the board to emit the recipe note. Every step after ingest sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output; nothing is loaded fresh mid-chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 21 steps, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning three connector groups (asset/stock, the raster photo family, and Firefly boards) plus one allowed local packaging step. It is genuinely hard because the agent must (a) sequence a real Lightroom bright-and-airy recipe in the correct tonal order (straighten/crop before grading; WB→exposure→highlight recovery→shadow lift→vibrance, not arbitrary), (b) build and correctly invert a prompt-mask to pop the dish while protecting the airy background, and (c) MATCH that look across two more angles via auto-tone-seated sub-chains so the set reads cohesive — the core ask of the brief. All steps are headless-confirmed [C] except one unavoidable [L] note-writing step; no [W]/[A]/[T] tools, no generative fill, no compositing, no background replacement, and image_fill_area is deliberately not used. Faithful to the real Upwork brief (bright-and-airy, white balance/exposure/color correction, flat-lay/45°/side, ASAP).

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capabilities anywhere. No generative expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no background replacement, no compositing. image_fill_area is deliberately NOT used. image_apply_lens_blur is not used. asset_preview_file/asset_inline_preview are not used; the record artifact (edit_recipe_note.txt) is emitted via the local recipe-note step, not by 'previewing into a new file'. The dangling asset_license_and_download_stock step and its unused linen_backdrop_ref have been removed, so no licensed-but-unconsumed asset remains and no stale prose references a stock backdrop or any styling composite.
reverify.notes: All 19 connector steps are server=='adobe' and map to [C] headless-confirmed raster/asset/board capabilities; the single trailing step 20 is the tolerated [L] local recipe-note writer (compiles the step log only, no core editing). Chaining: 19/19 steps after step 1 consume a prior named output via inputs_from (100%, >=80%). 15 distinct adobe tools (>=6); 20 total calls / 19 adobe calls (>=10). create_firefly_board presents already-produced CC deliverables. Selective food-pop uses the documented select_by_prompt -> invert_selection -> masked adjust_light_portions pattern. Client brand is invented/fictional; no real people, companies, or institutions depicted or named. Fully gen-free and Adobe-connector-doable.
